features.evidence: 1 storage but yeah it s been butter the whole time i am very happy to report now it s still a pixel so i have to add the disclaimer that it might not stay this way pixels have a history of this accelerated aging which is even more of a bummer when you pay this much for a phone but i can t report on that until we get to a year from now so the best i can do is tell you how it s acting now and it is acting great the other thing though that i m hoping changes over time is the battery life because the battery life in the first this week that i ve been testing the phone has not been great usually i can just throw away the first day or two of me testing this one because it s still setting it up still connecting everything and getting it working properly and then i can start looking at the numbers but every day since i first got this phone i ve had the same experience which is not that great four hours of screen on time at the end of my day which is not nearly as high as some of the flagships out there it is a 4 800 milliamp hour battery which is really good for a foldable but not gigantic for a giant screen on the inside so pixels have this feature called adaptive battery and sometimes they can get a little bit better over time depends on your use depends on who you are your mileage may vary but hey i ve gotten i would say this is c plus battery life and the more i think about it i ve been using a lot of the phone wall closed which means it s the smaller outside screen and so if i had been using the larger inside screen even more it would not have gone better so yeah c c plus for battery but i want to talk about the software experience here because it is a pixel after all and there is one weird thing so this was a very interesting phone to test because google had to do a lot of software work and i was excited to try all of it google s apps are all these beautiful tablet optimized apps now they re so thoughtfully designed for the phone with this size screen they all look normal on the outside screen but on the inside google drive has this nice slide out sidebar and multi column view gmail s new multi column view is great the weather app the new weather app is absolutely fantastic google calendar is completely redesigned there s just so much good stuff and then the new dock ties it all together so this dock i talked about on the pixel tablet you swipe up slightly from the bottom and the home bar turns into your home screen dock so this is the the apps that you have on the bottom of your home screen and you can quickly switch between these apps or launch into multitasking super easily so just the other folding phones i feel i feel i m multitasking god when i m using this huge folding phone open and just just type it away but here is the weird part as far as i can remember every other foldable phone allows you to set up the home screens and stuff on the outside screen and then the inside screen is separate you can have a separate set of widgets you can go crazy and make a different layout on the inside here and on this phone you cannot do that so look right now my outside screen is these three pages this one with the icons in the weather and then this calendar widget one and then this third one so when i open up the phone that s exactly mirrored here the left one is the first home screen then the right one is the second one and i swipe over and here s the third one but if i take this raindrop icon and move it over here and then when i go back to closed it s also moved the raindrop icon there because it s keeping them exactly mirrored at all times which is fine i think that makes a lot of sense especially for beginners i i bet if if apple made a folding iphone right now they would do the exact same thing because iphone users are used to not having a ton of customization on their home screens but i haven t found a way to unlock that at all there s no i haven t seen any home screen setting or display setting to be able to set up a different home screen on the inside versus the outside just feels on android to me i feel i can t be the only one who s spending eighteen hundred dollars on a first gen folding phone who would to have some apps on the outside screen and then a bunch of widgets on the inside screen you can t even span the middle with one widget because it s treating them as two separate home screens even though it s all with one big folding screen i just found that so weird so anyway aside from that i really enjoyed using the pixel fold seriously between the great performance and the google software all the pixel stuff google assistant call screening etc it s really easy to get past the weirdness the other thing you typically expect out of a pixel though is good cameras so let s talk about this camera situation for a little bit because as i ve said everywhere you look there s just a little bit of weird so first of all there are five cameras on this phone there is the nine megapixel hole punch camera on the front on the outside screen then there s an eight megapixel camera on the inside up in that bezel then there s the three primary cameras on the back the thing with these back cameras is because of how thin the phone is there s definitely not enough room to physically fit the same size sensors and optics that you have in the flagship pixel phone so there is all new stuff here and it s a little bit smaller so there s a 48 megapixel main camera a 10 megapixel ultra wide and a 10 megapixel 5x telephoto pixel fold photos feel a lot pixel 7a photos which isn t an insult that s just to say that they re in the same style the same contrasty pixel style but overall are definitely a step down in quality from the flagships they re a bit noisier a bit more muted in the colors especially with the ultra wide and it takes a bit longer to gather the same amount of light as the larger flagship sensors also if you want to see video from the pixel fold i shot the entire last autofocus video on the pixel fold so you can clearly see it s a step down from some of the other flagships i ve shot with there s a lot of noise and the darker areas of the frame but i will say it was definitely easier to shoot with because it s a folding phone so i can just unfold it and i see the viewfinder and i can frame things up with the primary camera so that s an advantage but yeah it s not you re gonna go out and shoot movies on this thing samsung has had to deal with the same type of thing the the camera quality of their super expensive foldable phones has always been a slight step down from their big flagship slab style phones so i think that s a fair comparison this is a step it s a notch down from the pixel flagship cameras what s fun this is a this is technically a first gen device right this is the first time google s ever made a foldable and on one hand you can tell that they ve at least learned some things from the other foldables already out there because this feels a little bit better in some ways they ve gone with this passport sized form factor and they ve made conscious decisions to make it thin and there s some hinge innovation here and it s kept the pixel aesthetic just a lot of good choices that are impressive for a first gen device but it s still clearly has some first gen shortcomings also that have me thinking well maybe the next one will be better for the inside display the crease and the bezels and the 178 degree almost flat fold maybe the next one will be better for the weak battery life yes there is wireless charging but hey maybe the next one will have faster charging than the current max of 30 watts and of course the app situation is still let s just say it s clearly imperfect so they ve done a lot of good stuff with their software but some apps still look horrible look at instagram it just it looks terrible you can double tap to the left or to the right to move it over closer to the side where it s more reachable but still that that is rough twitter also looks horrible my golf app 18 birdies also not optimized even youtube studio is just not optimized for this bigger screen and the list goes on and on and on maybe maybe that ll be better by the next one so then the last weird thing about this phone is it is one thousand eight hundred dollars so of course that that prices out pretty much anyone who s just gonna walk into a store casually and just get a normal upgrade for their phone it will be relegated to the early adopters the enthusiasts maybe people watching this video and the pixel fans it s a smaller group but also i gotta say weirdly enough i really using this phone i love using this phone it s got just the right set of quirks and features to that that i it over the regular pixel which i had been using and also i think it s it s specifically because it s so well optimized for using it closed i keep talking about how it s the best foldable to use closed it s got the right set of strengths and weaknesses that i spend most of my time just enjoying using it a normal phone close and then every once in a while my 20 30 40 of the time that i open it up and use it it s just this whole new world and it s a nice benefit it s getting closer to that ideal folding phone for me i really it it s also android 14 coming up soon five years of promise security updates great haptics great speakers this there s a lot of it s got just enough upside that i really it i m sure i m looking forward to the next one still of course but for anyone else who s going to jump in on this first gen version just know what you re getting into it s a weird one but i it also you might have spotted a few designs from channel sponsor dbrand a few times in this video this new one is called robot city and i think it s pretty sick it s one of my favorite designs they ve released yet it helps a lot also on a phone that says monotonous as the pixel fold so these designs have become a staple of dbrand over the years i don t know if you remember the the hair dryer that s been in the studio which was wrapped in the original robot skin so this new one robot city if you can look closely you ll see it there are three different versions that you can choose from there were all these unique discreet pieces of art first one is called dead quarters a dystopian headquarters of robot city the second one is amusement park complete with sharks and krakens and doomed carnival rides and the third one really hits home for me it s called crime square because times square but with crime there are so many easter eggs in them it s silly so if you want to check them out the robot city skins are available on over 100 devices including the pixel folds if you want to check them out i ll have a link down below in the description either way that s been it till the next one thanks for watching catch you later peace foreign

6 inch folding amoled display also 120 hertz and also plenty bright up to 1450 nits really nice size it s reminiscent of that oppo find n2 foldable but there are a couple of things to note here s where some of the weird starts so first of all the crease is pretty average it s definitely not dramatically worse than others i ve seen but you can for sure see it if you re looking for it and you can feel it but because it s through the middle of the screen you aren t usually running your finger over it unlike the vertically folding phones so it s not really a big deal it s easy to disappear to your eyes as you just look at content and use it normally but then there was just some talk about how it didn t seem the phone fully unfolds to 180 degrees and well i don t know this hinge is really firm so you can fold it up halfway or any angle you want really easily which is awesome but then when you fold it flat visually it looks pretty flat but when you put it on a table since there s a camera bump it becomes more clear as it s rocking back and forth it s maybe one or two degrees short of flat it s weird you can you can force it to get to flat or even a little bit past that but it really doesn t feel good to do that anyway then there is the bezel as you ve probably seen by now this phone does have a slightly bigger top and bottom bezel than some other folding phones it is noticeable at first when you just look at the bezels but again it s one of those things where you start using the phone and it is easy to forget about i think a lot of videos are going to hold it up side by side next to the samsung fold and then it s oh yeah clearly one of them has a dramatically thinner bezel and it is really noticeable but just using a phone it looks fine and google says that this was an intentional decision so what they were saying is they were again biasing so much towards it being thin that for some of the hinge components they move them out and above and below the hinge instead of in that z axis space so that it could be as thin as it is but now you end up with a little bit of extra bezel above oven below the screen but it did allow them room to put in a real webcam or selfie camera instead of a under display one but also i think this is a little bit along the lines of google the pixel has never really been all about pristine super highly engineered amazing hardware they ve flexed their strengths on the software side and i think there s a little bit of that here too either way you can check the box of ipx8 water resistance which is nice i do really this satin finish on the back which does a good job of minimizing fingerprints and looks pretty good and the camera bump doesn t go all the way to the very edges to meet the rails of the phone that would have been tough with the foldable but i do still think they did a good job of maintaining the whole pixel aesthetic performance is weirdly good it s it s really really good it s the best of any pixel phone even though it s using the same chip it s the tensor g2 from the pixel 7 and 7 pro but it s yeah it feels smoother and faster and more consistent than the pixel 7 and 7 pro so maybe it looks pretty good and the camera bump doesn t go all the way to the very edges to meet the rails of the phone that would have been tough with the foldable but i do still think they did a good job of and 7 pro but it s yeah it feels smoother and faster and more consistent than the pixel 7 and 7 pro so maybe it could be that 12 gigs of ram that are in here it also has 256 or 512 gigs of ufs 3 hey features.evidence: 7 inch 1440p 120 hertz oled display triple cameras and a 5 000 milliamp hour battery with 80 watt charging for 699 bucks that s pretty damn good so i think because the phone has the nice screen the big battery and the newest chip it begs comparisons to the 900 000 other flagship phones but i said it does cut some corners the big one being no wireless charging huge and then the other it s ip64 rated instead of ip67 so it ll survive a mist better than a splash but on the design front i d call this a mixed bag mixed bag it definitely is taking a step away from the ultra generic last generation the hasselblad branding is back and we ll talk about the cameras in a minute but also the alert slider is back literally an iconic oneplus feature that they somehow thought getting rid of last time was a good idea this one also has a very slightly textured matte black finish on the back not quite sandstone level but enough to be interesting so i it and the camera bump the camera bump is a weird hybrid of smoothly wrapping around the side samsung used to but then turning it into a big circle plateau that looks a generic not seamless thing anymore at all it s whatever i don t mind it it s fine but you re not here for the back of the phone though honestly if you just saw the back and saw the lack of wireless charging and a slightly awkward camera bump it feels a 699 phone sure but the front is where it gets way more impressive 6

now people who have been following the industry for a little bit already know the story behind this phone which is oneplus starting out hot as a fan favorite with a plus bang for the buck flagship killer type of phones peaking i d say around the oneplus 7 pro it s my phone of the year and then slowly descending into the very generic expensive flagship that they swore to kill their last attempt at a phone the 10t is literally the worst phone that came out last year it won my bust of the year and i got a trophy for it and apparently they ve used this as motivation they keep saying oh they now they want to redeem themselves now they re gonna turn it around and great i think we all know the next phone was already in the works by the time they got that trophy but if you want to give me some credit i ll take some credit sure but the point is they re reflecting shades of old oneplus which is a really good thing because this phone is really good so this is a flagship smartphone right now for 6

features.evidence: great screen, great battery, great build, great performance, great camera, and i m not being generous with that stuff

because, i this phone, and i m gonna go ahead and guess that some other reviewers will this phone too, and some enthusiasts will this phone, and this video may even get a lot of views, but none of that necessarily translates into regular people just, strolling into a store, or going online and choosing to buy one of these
so this is a super high end phone in every way, and the design is particularly well considered
3 inch, 20 by nine cover display, just over 1080p
but there s no question this feels a flagship phone on the outside, even if you never even open the thing
i think it adds, no cost and no weight and no thickness, but yet they leave it out when all the other competitors have it anyway
but then the software for this one is oneplus

2 on top of it and it s got a nice level of customization it s not the best i ve ever seen but definitely enough to make the phone yours and it s just generally a well animated nicely tactile experience any list or web page you flick between you don t just see it you feel it and you also can t not notice putting aside the stability problems for a second the core decisions being made for the day to day user experience they are very focused around it just being intuitive at the very start i was struggling to reach the unlock pattern in the middle then i realized that these two buttons on either side they move the user interface towards whichever hand is holding the phone i felt it was hard to press the power button and the volume buttons at the same time to take screenshots but then realized that a quick swipe with three fingers it does the same thing too it does also do that whole thing where it remaps some apps when you re in the half folded position it s not as fleshed out as samsung s flex mode but for me there s not really a huge use case for either the main thing this needs is polish and the fact that oneplus is now promising four full years of major android updates is promising it s likely that they ll iron out the bugginess but you can t guarantee it so at this point overall the oneplus open is doing pretty well well and we haven t even talked about its crown jewel the cameras the thing that oneplus has really zeroed in on with the marketing here is that most foldable phones have compromised cameras you have to fit those cameras into what s effectively half the depth of a normal flagship and do what they re not wrong about that and what they re also not wrong about is that this phone in response to that is as kitted out as it gets this open has a triple camera hassleblad branded setup consisting of a 48 map main camera 48 map ultra wide camera and 64 megapix three times tel photo camera and megapixels are one thing but these are also all large sensor cameras each respectively probably the biggest you can find in a foldable and with the main camera they re using a new pixel stacking technique that they re saying allows the phone to get in even more light than the numbers suggest the pitch that oneplus gave to me was that this is not just the best set of cameras unfoldable but that this is the best set of cameras on a smartphone now that s a pretty exciting prospect and doubly exciting cuz when you improve the cameras on a foldable you re not just improving your rear photos but your selfies too cu on the front you also have access to all three of these cameras in full quality and in some ways oneplus has delivered the ultra wide camera is great in fact i take better ultra wide shots on this than i do on my iphone 15 pro max the telephoto camera being a 64 megapixel camera means that while its optical zoom is only three times you can go all the way to six times and still get a lossless 12 megap image it still gives you that feeling of oh there s something really far away don t worry my phone will be able to see it yeah i was really excited about my veggie fingers now they do advertise this as going up to 120x ultra res zoom but let me tell you at 120x this is anything but ultra r and it s fine that it can t do this no one s expecting it to but can we please stop pretending vast amounts of digital zoom is a selling point the only potential exception to this is patterns if you zoom in 120x to a pattern where the exact specifics of what you re shooting don t really matter then the camera can do a really good job of almost drawing in the detail that does not work for faces i ve taken some awesome selfies selfies that just wouldn t have been possible using any other phone just from the fact that this folds and it s all bundled together with some fairly pleasant color signs thanks to hassleblad photos are a little darker than i d have liked but generally what s good is that you re never getting faces that are tomato red or trees so green they look radioactive it s quite grounded in reality it s really good at resisting being overexposed on the main camera and it sounds good this is one of the nicest shutter experiences that i ve had in recent memory but you ve probably realized where i m going with this i don t think it lives up to its potential and it s not the hardware the hardware is great it s just oneplus s camera software not being as intelligent to some of the competition you ll notice it with portrait mode it s not the best at chopping people s hair for example and it s super sensitive to movement and easy to blur if you re not still to be fair with normal photos you have an action mode which is the exact opposite and really hard to blur but it s just a shame that you can t have it all i shouldn t have to go into an action mode to take photos that i can trust are blur free and the ability to use ai to extract detail from your photos which to me is one of the most important things is not as good as the new iphones by a long shot and the video too is on the weaker end samsung and google this phone is quite susceptible to grain in conditions that aren t really that dark and you ll also notice especially when recording on either of the front cameras which are the default for taking selfie video they are quite easy to overexpose the overall message here is that this is a good camera system it s the best camera system on aordable it s just that oneplus is definitely overshot with their claims because what it definitely isn t is the best phone camera outright the one unique thing that oneplus has is xan which produces these super wide very cinematic looking shots it s a fun gimmick and it even comes with its own shooting user interface which is meant to mimic the traditional hassleblad experience but really all this is doing is taking a wide shot and chopping off the top and the bottom at that point i d rather just take a panorama and get better quality there s not much to say about the displays apart from the fact that they are the creme lae foldables it bothers me slightly that the black bezel on this side is not symmetrical with the other bezels but apart from that they ve nailed the ratio of width to height so this is a 20 to9 aspect ratio display which is the exact same as the company s normal flagships both screens are super bright and using ltpo 3
snapdragon 8 gen 2, 16 gigs of fast ram, 512 gigs of ufs 4

this is the oneplus open it s the first ever oneplus foldable and honestly the first oneplus product in the last 5 years that s really impress me see foldable phones up until this point for most people including myself haven t really made sense you pay more money almost double the price of a flagship to get a phone that has a bigger screen yes but then it s compromised in almost every other way in terms of comfort they re very thick when folded in terms of cameras they struggle to fit good ones in you get hinge problems you get crease problems you get dust stuck in your screen problems there s 50 more examples of this but this oneplus open is different you can tell it s not just trying to be yet another companies take at the same thing but the foldable done right paying attention to all of the pain points you normally get and trying to fix them so let s talk about it the unboxing is a good start you get everything you need with it which shouldn t be a surprise for a 1,699 phone but that s just where we are right now the phone s on top and that is a proper camera crater you get a 67 w fast charg it is starting to feel a little dated that it s a traditional usba a plug and not usbc though and then a second really heavy box there s a case included for both the back and the front it s nothing crazy fancy but it is all the protection that 90 of people are going to need and then inside a cable classic oneplus red and then welcome membership card quick guide and then a safety guide why is the safety guide 60 pages what is this 1995 and then a simage tool time for the big peel now this hasn t been one of those experiences where i ve had to dig to find the differences while it still looks and feels familiar if you ve seen a foldable before the improvements to the foldable formula are really obvious but first thing i ll notice is how slim and light it is mostly thanks to new materials in the body titanium instead of steel is it the most comfortable phone i ve ever held hell no but is it close enough to feeling a normal phone that you could just maybe forget yeah i d say so to give you some idea this foldable with an 8 in screen on the inside is the same weight as an iphone 14 pro max which i have carried around for a year with no problems but that is not the only interesting thing going on here the hinge the hinge on foldable phones is both one of the biggest opportunities and potential vulnerabilities and i m just going to say it right now this is the best hinge i ve ever seen it s very smooth there s zero friction but at the same time at any point in the opening process if you want to stop you stop and then according to oneplus it s also a lot more durable than any other hinge before thanks to being made from no joke exactly 69 parts as opposed to most hinges which have over 100 and so they re saying are almost over engineered there s more the hinge makes for a gapless design all the way around when it s closed which keeps dust and other substances out but what s also cool is that theyve managed to do that while not making the hinge protrude outwards and while also giving the screen enough space to rest when folded such that it s not being pinched and so when you unfold it there is very very minimal crease i ve used a lot of foldable phones at this point and in most cases the crease is just one of those side characters that you really dislike at the start and then you just have to get used to always being around but here what you have is a screen with traces of a crease it s minimal enough that if you choose to see it you ll see it but if you choose to ignore it you absolutely can and you re left with what genuinely does feel a mini tablet on the inside but the best bit and the thing that really helps to sell this feeling is this the way that most foldables open is very gradually with a consistent amount of resistance until you decide okay this seems fully open i ll stop pushing now which would probably be the way to do it if you sometimes use a foldable this sometimes this sometimes this but for being very honest 95 of the time you use your foldable really you re going to be in this position or you re going to be in this position and that is why i really love the fact that this phone snaps into both of those positions it s satisfying it leaves no shadow of a doubt that you are in the fully open or fully closed position and i subconsciously it also helps that feeling of distinction okay i m in phone mode now i m in tablet mode that s how i would make a foldable there s also an alert slider which used to be a classic oneplus feature they ditched it for a while and now it s finally back so that s exciting it s fine it does what an alert slider does but it did need to be about 5 cm lower for me to realistically reach it there s ipx for water resistance which is good for splashes the point i was going to make though is that it s not rated for full on immersion samsung s foldables are and then the camera which for some people i ve showed this to is massively annoying and intrusive but for me it s been okay my fingers just rest around it and because it s at least centered and symmetrical i found that it s got some but minimal wobble when i m using it on my desk so overall the design is great but design alone is not going to fix foldables one of the biggest questions i and many people have is how do you use the extra space you have in a way that makes this better than a normal phone and the software on the oneplus open i would say is the most convincing answer to that question that i ve ever seen so the screen is this big right well think of it it s this big meaning that while of course you can open an app and have it exist here and then you can open another app and have it exist here you can now also open an app and have it exist here the apps will automatically open to whatever ratio makes the most sense for them and not necessarily just what will fit your screen and when you open a new one it will just occupy a new area within this bigger virtual canvas and the first time that i saw this i just sat there nodding my head yes a sudden moment of of course this is how you get around the fact that you have a square display when most apps are designed for rectangles this is how you make this bigger screen feel tangibly more useful and this is how you multitask in a way that doesn t feel so formal and restrictive and it s all assisted by this doc which is your fixed apps that you set in the middle your recent apps on the right and then on the left an app draw and the best bit a recent files folder so a combination of stuff photos you ve just taken and zip files you ve just downloaded and you don t really need to think about this stock it s normal size when you re on the home screen and then whenever you open an app it just shrinks down just about still being useful without being an eyesa and then if you ve got a ton happening at once and you just want to get rid of it you hold down and it goes now if you re not coming from a foldable phone there is still absolutely a learning curve here but then when you get going no foldable has ever made as much sense to me is this one right here so here s an example use case from a couple of days ago i was playing a game and in that game there s a bit of a wait time between rounds so i thought i m going to use this time to find a new wallpaper so two finger swiping down the middle will split the app and allow you to click on a different one to open alongside it so i was playing the game while browsing for wallpapers until i found the perfect one that i was going to use but then i thought well i the style of the wallpaper but what would be even cooler as if i match the color of that wallpaper to the color of this phone so one tap on this dock scroll to the rec color app drag that into the fray and now we ve got all three apps open at once and if i want to transfer the wallpaper to that recolor app i literally just drop it in and we re away editing it all felt very seamless but how is it possible partly the high end snapdragon 8 gen 2 chipset partly the fact that you get 512 gigs of fast fast storage and largely due to the 16 gb of topend ram you get here this thing is the most well equ equipped phone hardware that money can buy enough to run any single app you could possibly want when focusing on it enough to run three fairly intensive applications at the same time and then one more as a floating window and then if you re only doing light work enough to keep literally 40 apps open in the background at any one time so almost nothing needs to reload when you open it later on and in my testing it s also able to do this while keeping cool thanks in part to a sheet of ultra conductive graphine going across the body which is not just good at getting heat from inside to the outside but also heat from one side of the body to the other it spreads it so that if one part of your phone gets really hot it s if effectively diluting that heat across the entire width of your phone so it s less hot now three pretty major caveats to this one if you re someone who even with this capability is still just going to be using one app at a time which let s be honest is probably still most people then i don t think this is any more the phone for you as any other foldable you d be missing out on a lot of what s different about it two that even with all of this power here while it is very smooth when focusing on one thing as soon as you start to juggle the lag can creep in which i imagine is partly because it s really stretching the capability of what a phone chip is designed to do but also partly that there s a lot of new stuff here and it needs optimizing on oneplus s end and then three speaking of optimizing in its current state this is pretty buggy stuff this it happens often i imagine it s because what oneplus is allowing you to do is so wide open and free form that it s very easy to run into situations that haven t been accounted for i get it but then spend more time accounting for it and there s also quite a lot of things on the android end that need work some apps that really should be supporting this whole dragon dropping a files between them not supporting it but okay that should not completely take away from the fact that there is a lot to commend here the fact that oneplus only has one model of this phone that comes with 16 gigs of ram and 512 gigs of storage by default it s very easy to create a 128 gig model with 8 gigs of ram to make the advertised price seem way more affordable and then just incentivize upgrades but they just decided no the full foldable experience requires this amount of ram and storage that s the minimum amount that we re going to sell it with and they re doing that while still undercutting the samsung the oneplus open is 1699 versus samsung 1799 and that s for a phone that has 512 gigs of storage versus 256 and 16 gigs of ram instead of 12 it s still very expensive but at least you re getting the best of the best hardware for that price these foldables right now they are far too expensive for what they are so even though i don t think we re quite there yet at least this is putting some downwards pressure on that price the actual software version here is android 13 with oneplus s oxygen os 13

review_summary: great screen, great battery, great build, great performance, great camera, and i m not being generous with that stuff because, i this phone, and i m gonna go ahead and guess that some other reviewers will this phone too, and some enthusiasts will this phone, and this video may even get a lot of views, but none of that necessarily translates into regular people just, strolling into a store, or going online and choosing to buy one of these so this is a super high end phone in every way, and the design is particularly well considered

features.evidence: okay so today we have the newly launched oneplus note 3 with us now we have already given our first impression of this phone and there we set this then the oneplus note 3 should be under 30 000 rupees at that price it is a good phone however if it is priced above that say at 35 000 rupees then oneplus is own oneplus 11r at 40 000 is a far far better offering and well guess what the note 3 did launch for 33 triple nine and they have better cameras in the indian variant but this oneplus 11r is also a good option so the question that needs to be asked is is not three a good choice or should you pay a little extra and go for the oneplus 11r well to answer this question i ve been using both the phones for quite a few days now and i must say things are very interesting here see this won t be a first impression it s bit of an early review i ll give you more practical side of things which we experienced in the three to five days so stay tuned till the end of the video let s go routine stuff you get this box interesting thing is the box is classic black with this steel patch of nord inside you get the phone some paperwork this exclusive red cable club card back cover it s silicon 80 watt charger type a to type c red charging cable and this emoji style sim ejector tool so everything you need comes with the phone in the box that s nice now there are three main interesting things with the note 3 and by the way we also have the oneplus 11r with us so wherever necessary i ll also mention the difference number one is design see the note 3 is a good balance of boxy and curved phones on the back the glass is slightly curved at the edges so it doesn t hurt your fingers while holding it on the front it has a flat display so getting a screen protector for this would be easy my only concern with the design is the back you can see the fingerprints as well as it is slippery i was using the note 3 in bed casually watching videos and it would occasionally start to slip out of my fingers and fall and that s not all or not three the back glass has gorilla glass 5 protection but on the front you get dragon trail protection the back has better protection than the front let us know in the comment section why do you think they did that either way i would recommend using a back cover the one that comes included in the box it is good enough now design choice is very this objective and though the note 3 does feel good in the hand but see side by side with the oneplus 11r i would personally prefer the oneplus 11r due to the more premium feel and the curved display curved back feels premium in the hand second would be the display see the note 3 comes with a 6

if i go to google chrome again it is 60 and if i go to youtube still 60 and you will feel the difference when you are scrolling the apps feel a bit jittery and we did the same thing on the oneplus 11r as well but see here on play store it is 120 hertz and even in chrome if you scroll it is 90 hertz so i think the refresh rate can be better optimized so the refresh rate on the note 3 is being capped to save battery or just bad optimization so you ll have to go to display set refresh rate to high now that jitteriness problem would be fixed now the third thing is camera and here s the interesting thing both oneplus 11r and not 3 have the same exact camera specs you get a 50 megapixel main camera 8 megapixel ultra wide and of course of course 2 megapixel macro camera now we took quite a few pictures it will not three while we re using it for two to three days it takes decent photo even low light the pictures come out fine however things get interesting when you put oneplus 11r side by side so see these pictures from the main camera at first glance both the picture look fine there s a slight difference in color tone but if you zoom in see the 11r has more details have a look around my beard it is more sharp and it happens with every picture this one zoom in oneplus 11r has more clarity more sharpness even in low light see the pictures with baby oda again if you zoom in on the cheek area the 11r has more details coming to the ultrawide you get an 8 megapixel ultra wide on both so the picture quality looks okay coming to videos both the phones can do 4k 60fps but again if you see the oneplus 11r footage don t looks more stable now both the phones have a 16 megapixel front selfie camera and both can record only up to 1080p 30 fps come on chandrayaan 3 has launched it s 2023 and we still can t do 4k recording from the front camera what is even in the selfies see the picture on note 3 is good it s not bad but side by side the 11 hours picture has more clarity now speaking of software both the phones have the same oxygen os 13 for starters this is a very clean android experience there is almost no bloatware or any ads almost because the only thing that i encountered is you get this internet or oppo browser which cannot be uninstalled at max you can disable it also when you search something in the app drawer you see this recommendation of apps thankfully it only leads to the google play store now this happened both on the note 3 and oneplus 11r so i m not sure i think this happened after an update let us know if it happens with your phone but if you have to disable it you can just go to settings disable smart suggestions comparatively i would say it s far better than what real me and oppo does in color os additionally you get android 13 right out of the box and oneplus is promising three years of os update and four years of security update which is pretty good also a special mention to the haptics now i can t show you this on camera but there is a difference between types of vibration if i m scrolling on the app switcher the vibration is subtle but if i m typing on the keyboard the vibration is a bit more intense the haptics are well optimized in the os and you get the same haptics on the note 3 and oneplus 11r which is a nice thing coming to performance well the note 3 comes with the dimension 9000 processor starts from 8gb ram 128gb storage we have the base variant and in normal day to day usage taking phone calls browsing the web casual gaming and all phone is fine of course it costs 34 000 there is no lag or anything we even played a few rounds of bgmi the gameplay was okay and here s an interesting thing see on 92 in the default mode it shows about 7 lakh 87 700 but now when you go into settings there is this high performance mode so i ll enable it and run the same end to do benchmark again now it scores more than 9 lakh but here s the point should phone automatically switched to performance mode when needed now again see we played this heavy game carx so this is a very good game you can see the shadows and graphics it s very rich and dynamic so now we ll do a time trial and let s go and see the overall game has a nice amount of lag you can see the graphics and rendering in fact see it shows about 18 fps 19 fps which is very low at least it should be 30 fps and let me try and hit someone see the lag see the frame drops and all so if you re a heavy game plus 11r come with 5000 mah battery under normal conditions casual gaming browsing the web and all of that they should last you around one day besides the note 3 supports 80 watt charging and the oneplus 11r supports 100 watt charging so 11 arc in charge slightly faster but no big deal also they have all the important sensors nfc and also you get an ir blaster so you can control your tv or ac whatever works with an ir which brings me to the burning question should you consider the note 3 at 34 000 or should you pay a little extra and get the oneplus 11r so i ll give a very simple answer listen carefully at 34 000 the note 3 is a bit expensive if you can get it around 30 31 000 with deals and offer that s a good price but at 34 000 icon neo 7 pro is a far better option than better performance and camera and if you need a oneplus phone i would recommend spend a bit more and get the oneplus 11r that has better build better display better camera and a more stable performance i hope this clear things up if it did on that note this predict signing off see you in the next pew

so everyone s been talking about the crazy oneplus n 3 deal on flip cart geom but amazon did a reverse uno and now the oneplus n 3 is available on amazon at 199 so no more doubts no more checking warranties amazon is the official oneplus reseller yeah it all started making sense on paper this seems the better phone compared to the n c4 the nothing phone 28 the moto h50 fusion so it seems a no brainer to just get this eyes closed rewind it seems seems if you look at the amazon reviews of the oneplus n 3 the primary concern is the heating issues and the battery life so the question is is this hot and not in a good way look i ve been using this phone as my daily driver to find out the truth and let me make it clear that there s a lot of killer things in the n 3 at this price the design is great it s matte finished gorilla glass five back looks and feels great the frame is plastic and that s fine because it s not too heavy it s pretty sleek it s got the alert slider which is enough to make oneplus fans come you don t get the alert slide and oneplus phones in this segment so this is awesome now coming to the features no headphone jack or micro sd slot but other than that this is packed really good studo speakers with dolby atmos support really good haptics for a phone in this segment definitely better than say the h50 fusion dual sim slots 5g support with all the bands carrier aggregation no issues with net network calls a very fast in display fingerprint scanner i blaster on the top the ip54 rating which is okay you get the 80w charger in the box you get this case too and the phone even comes with a screen protector applied i said packed the screen is also high in amate panel 1

doesn t hurt your fingers while holding it on the front it has a flat display so getting a screen protector for this would be easy my only concern with the design is the back you can see the fingerprints as well as it is slippery i was using the note 3 in bed casually watching videos and it would occasionally start to slip out of my fingers and fall and that s not all or not three the back glass has gorilla glass 5 protection but on the front you get dragon trail protection the back has better protection than the front let us know in the comment section why do you think they did that either way i would recommend using a back cover the one that comes included in the box it is good enough now design choice is very this objective and though the note 3 does feel good in the hand but see side by side with the oneplus 11r i would personally prefer the oneplus 11r due to the more premium feel and the curved display curved back feels premium in the hand second would be the display see the note 3 comes with a 6

features.evidence: this is xiaomi s brand new 14 ultra and after almost 14 years of xiaomi phones having the same old mui software this is where they reinvent themselves the phone spec sheet is absolutely jacked it s got new ai features some of them genuinely mind blowing it s got their nextgen hyper os operating system and it s arguably also the most decked out camera system on the planet so in the 2024 smartphone market how does xiaomi s best compare to samsung s best the phone that i ve been using every day since launch the galaxy s 24 ultra well for starters the unboxing experience is just leagues ahead for three reasons so we got an insert on top which has the sim ejector tool but more importantly one a frosted plastic hard shell case it s nothing fancy but it s functional assuming that you re going to buy a decent quality case for your phone online anyways then it s a 20 saving and ironically better for the environment you got the phone obviously and a cable every phone comes with but this also has number two a 90 w charging that s two times the maximum power that samsung is able to charge with and the final thing is well when you peel the phone you will also notice a pre installed screen protector which as with the chargers samsung does not come with don t get me wrong screen protectors extremely cheap but the main benefit from it being pre installed is that let s be honest the company are going to do a better job than we are and we re not done yet there s a whole separate photography kit which is this pretty indul package containing a nicer looking textured case this one is leather with a specific coating that highly resists bacteria and dirt it s got a mount that allows you to attach physical filters and that also looks a slot for a lanyard it s all a bit excessive for most people but i can t deny it s cool these two are just decorative camera rings to style your weird phone camera hybrid as you please and then this is a grip for that case which plugs into your usbc port to give you proper hardware camera controls as well as three very cool features which will get to and then oh there s the lanyard but even putting all this photography kit stuff aside cuz it is a separate purchase just the core package contents of the xiaomi do wipe the floor with what the samsung comes with so that s one point to no points but then as soon as i picked the xiaomi up i i started laughing don t get me wrong there is absolutely merit to xiaomi s design i really these leather finishes it feels clean and grippy and hassle free and also having a central symmetrical camera is nice and practical for using the thing on a flat surface however these cameras have become so big so deep and so weighty that it feels they ve started to invade the core phone experience to the point where most of the people i ve handed this to it s the first thing they notice and they are completely put off by it especially compared to the samsung which is so great for bright hued almost any position and still avoid the cameras to quote joseph in my team here it feels you re holding holding a piece of tech stuck onto a piece of tech and while i can tolerate it i m always someone who wants to make sure i m using the best tech there is and i m willing to be a little bit uncomfortable to do so this right here is my issue this phone is so topheavy i genuinely feel i m having to exert extra grip strength just to keep it in my hands you feel the urge every single time you pick it up to hold it higher up than usual to balance out that weight distribution but you can t do that because the camera protrude so much and takes up half the phone and so you just end up with this continuous sense of dredge knowing that at any time one wrong hand over and it s a vista it gets in the way of wireless charging the camera lifts the phone up so high that in quite a few situations it no longer makes proper contact with the charger or the phone that you re trying to charge with it if you re reverse wireless charging i really needed this thing last week to save my dying samsung while i was out traveling and about an hour into delicately fiddling with these two phones trying to perfectly match the coils to get charge from here to here while avoiding this massive camera module the size of the moon i just wanted to chop the thing off also this bezel on the back i hate it clearly this whole thin on the top thick on the sides thing is a purposeful decision that xia s made but i just don t understand why cuz to me it just looks they took this leather strip and misapplied it so pretty clear point for samsung with the build and the usability which makes it 1 1 so far but then you turn the phones over and you ll see that xiaomi s done something pretty unique with the screen a long running complaint that i ve had with their past phones is they ve always had this thicker bottom bezel and these weird bunny rabbit looking corners but in one phone they have fixed both of those things and taken it further they re calling this an allaround liquid display which is indicative of well a how companies are running out of ways to describe glass and pixels but b how the panel melts into the edges and the corners equally in all directions it s subtle but i think it s a really aesthetic effect it s how 10 years ago we used to sketch out what we d imagine our future phones would look and it s definitely cooler than samsung s flat display it s more unique and it s more interesting but that doesn t make it better both are top of their game in most aspects bright a high refresh rate that can fully adapt on the fly and ultra high resolution but i would side slightly with samsung for this one because for gestures you have to use the very edges of the screen and that ends up being on the curve for xiaomi so it doesn t register quite as reliably two that even though samsung s max brightness is technically rated lower in all the lighting conditions i ve tested it in it seems to be brighter brightness is a weird stat these days and then what seals it for me is this anti reflective coating that samy slipped onto the s24 ultra it is so good that you almost forget about the surrounding light and that makes your screen experience way more legible as if you had even higher brightness but without the extra battery drain that comes with that which leads us to a score of 1 2 now but we all know that more and more every single year the battle for the best smartphone it s a software battle way more than it is a hardware battle so let s talk about that and because it s such a massive category now we re splitting it into three user interface software support and ai okay you might remember xiaomi s mii it s the software skin they ve had on every phone ever since the company first started but i m guessing that since mi s reputation was always fairly mid and potentially holding their hero flagship phones back they finally decided that it s time for a redo a new skin called hyper os with a ton of new features new animations greater efficiency and built to work particularly well with other xiaomi products so yeah we re talking about the first full software remake from a company whose entire foundation was based on its software now that sounds awesome which is why i was slightly underwhelmed when i booted it up for the first time and realized well that just looks my last xiaomi phone and that s okay i ve never hated the way that xiaomi s os looks but it is also far from my favorite right now it very much feels it s trying to be ios but just without the attention to detail or the consistency of ios and i particularly do not appreciate the built in adverts cuz you would almost assume given that hyper os is meant to be xiaomi s big breakaway from mii and its advert filled reputation that this would be the opposite their way to overcorrect and to make it right but that does not appear to be the case the mus have recommend mended apps that it shows you before you go to xiaomi s app store how it takes just one swipe from your lock screen and you re in an ad it calls itself a wallpaper carousel but really it s an ad in disguise cuz as soon as you click on anything you will be taken to a page with so many popups it genuinely makes you want to throw up even on the home screen one swipe and you see news but it s news in the worst possible way to consume news with two large banner ads on screen at any point in time and that s on top of the fact that you already have the google news feed with which does take you to the sites that you want to go to oh but the worst is the notifications why why on earth does the mi browser need to specifically ping me to tell me i need to see this random article again with about 40 adverts baked in now of course these are all easy to avoid and you can disable almost all instances of advertising in the phone so i don t think it s a reason to not buy it but i just don t the feeling that clearly to xiaomi i am as well as the customer i m also the product that they can sell to their advertisers okay so that was my pretty bleak first impression of the software but the more i ve used it the better it has got it for starters you realize that hyper os is ultra lightweight just for example samsung s os takes up around 17 gb of your data this is less than 10 and within that there is still a little bit of blow wear but the good news is you now have complete control over it anything on the phone is uninstallable the lock screen customization has now opened up and it not just allows you to instantly nail one type of look really well but a whole suite of very different aesthetics with also room to add your own personal touches to them it reminds me of the variation that would have previously required you to use third party apps and workarounds but it s built in and that makes it work so much smoother but it is still lacking on the lock screen widgets front they ve cleaned up the control center when you swipe down just ios they ve got rid of all the text that tells you what each icon is which means it takes a little bit of getting used to but once you re used to it it feels clean now you can fit more icons on screen and instead of swiping between pages you just scroll down now there s new animations and the whole thing is really fluid i only really clocked this on my third full day of using the phone but it was a sudden moment of realization of wait a sec have i had a single bit of lag in my entire experience no it feels reliably almost unbreakables do even when you haven t opened an app in ages and you expect it to stutter a little bit nothing or this is the camera app relative to most camera apps this is slick there s new gallery features some very quirky portrait mode effects probably a little too quirky for me but they re fun oh and also cutout which can instantly just chop your background out or swap it for something else hyper os also has this dynamic island feature on top for little things charging and silent mode and i think it s done pretty well because of the screen s ability to portray deep blacks it genuinely feels your phone bezel itself is extending down to create this separate area within your screen it s a convincing illusion but it is also early days something this is only useful in so much as there are apps that support it and well not many apps support it so yeah hyper os is a good step in the right direction it is better than the mii it s replacing but i still prefer using samsung s 1 ui i think this new version of samsung s os it feels a really good balance of playful but mature i how you can swipe your notifications away both ways as opposed to xiaomi s one way or how you can expand those notifications in all cases with a little swipe down that feels so intuitive to me the customization is absolutely absurd thanks to samsung s goodlock suite of apps and you can do it all without any need to get into the nitty gritty of installing custom firmware and the vibrations samsung s vibration motor is so good that you forget there is a vibration motor it almost fuses the hardware and the software so that typing for example feels really tactile and you don t even immediately know why now that is not quite the case with xiaomi so i wouldn t call this a landslide win for samsung both phones will absolutely do everything you need them to do and more but it s just i think samsung has improved this generation more than xiaomi has with hyper os even though xiaomi is supposedly the one who s had the big rebrand and the whole thing with the ads in the software while it isn t a deal breaker for me it does leave a bad taste in the mouth but hey i ve waited through them for the last 2 weeks to make this video so a sub to the channel would wow me okay here s the thing with xiaomi though one of the biggest complaints that s often leveled at them is the software support in recent years samsung has massively revamped theirs they went from promising 2 years of major android updates to 4 years and now 7 years on this phone this s24 ultra will get updates until android version 21 and that s not a cheap thing to do i was talking to someone i know in the phone industry someone who makes the decisions for how many updates their phones get and they were telling me that they spend an average of a million per major update per model of phone they want to get those updates for and this is where xiaomi does save a bit of cost there s a fair few of their flagship phones in the past that they have completely just left high and dry which was extremely sad it s better now they re offering four full years which not that long ago to be fair would have seemed incredible but hey the benchmark keeps moving and xiaomi is consistently catching up so this one goes to samsung too let s talk artificial intelligence with the launch of the s24 ultra it felt one of those mic drop ai is here moments there s around 10 different ai features all weaved into various parts of the software and all available on day one i d say about half of them are gimmicks fun party tricks that are cool to try but you won t really use after the first week and half of them are either already game changers or they have a good chance to become game changers in one way way the entirety of hyper os is built around the ability to use ai there s this big incoming feature called hypermind which apparently uses ai to understand your needs and then execute things automatically across all your xiaomi products but hypermind is coming in a few weeks apparently as of right now at this point in time there aren t nearly as many discrete ai features on the xiaomi as samsung but that doesn t mean there aren t any for one hidden away in the gallery is an absolutely enormous ai feature called video effects you pick a clip and then you just tap and hold on whatever effects you want to apply to that clip and it automatically tracks them to that person in that footage there was a time not very long ago at all where this would have to be drawn in by hand and it would take a full day just to edit a 30 second video so the fact that i can now completely without internet literally tap and hold my screen to edit an effect into the video for however long i want feels i have just been granted a superpower and if i was a little better at the robot this would look absolutely awesome i tried okay it can even use its awareness of where you are to place text behind you which is a complex effect that if you re doing it manually with a traditional video editing program it takes way longer and requires much more skill than it does here on your smartphone there are some ai features baked into the camera too but i ll get to the camera separately for this ai category i would say samsung still takes it just for the sheer breadth of ai features but the fact that what xiaomi has done they have done so well it does get me excited for what might be next oh dear the scorecard is not looking very good for xiaomi right now but how does the performance measure up well both phones have the snapdragon 8 gen 3 processor with samsung s advantage being that they always get custommade versions just for them that are slightly overclocked and specifically optimized and xiaomi s perk that this goes up to 16 gb of ram instead of samsung 12 so if we re talking numbers samsung is a little higher on cpu and a pretty decent amount higher on graphics xiaomi is going to be able to keep a few more apps suspended without having to force close them but i tell you the much starker difference between these two is the thermals thermals i would say are even more important than a bit of extra ram or a chip being made for galaxy because in theory it only matters if your phone is powerful if it can be powerful while staying cool and xiaomi does not seem to be very good at doing that check this out so this is the wildlife extreme stress test it puts the phones through 30 minutes of pure pain and in that samsung s internal temperature goes up to 44 xia s 52 samsung feels way cooler too on the outside whereas this especially on these metal rails on the sides it feels you could cook an egg on there here s the really weird part of it though i have run four of these tests now and every single time xiaomi while always getting hotter also manages to keep its performance higher there s way less of a drop off after long strain which is good but considering that i can see and i can absolutely feel that it s getting hotter is also unusual and maybe not good the way i m interpreting that result it s it s almost xiaomi is being less conservative with protecting the chip and the battery in the long term allowing the phone to go full throttle and stay at full throttle even if potentially reducing its lifespan so i ll give performance a draw just because who wins really depends on what you re prioritizing but where i think you feel this looser power management more directly is battery everything about xiaomi s battery experience sounds it should be better both phones have 5,000 mah batteries but xiaomi s wired charging is 90 w of power versus samsung s 45 xiaomi s wireless charging is 80 ws versus samsung s 15 and xiaomi s ability to reverse charge other phones is 10 w versus samsung s 4

notice a pre installed screen protector which as with the chargers samsung does not come with don t get me wrong screen protectors extremely cheap but the main benefit from it being pre installed is that let s be honest the company are going to do a better job than we are and we re not done yet there s a whole separate photography kit which is this pretty indul package containing a nicer looking textured case this one is leather with a specific coating that highly resists bacteria and dirt it s got a mount that allows you to attach physical filters and that also looks a slot for a lanyard it s all a bit excessive for most people but i can t deny it s cool these two are just decorative camera rings to style your weird phone camera hybrid as you please and then this is a grip for that case which plugs into your usbc port to give you proper hardware camera controls as well as three very cool features which will get to and then oh there s the lanyard but even putting all this photography picked the xiaomi up i i started laughing don t get me wrong there is absolutely merit to xiaomi s design i really these leather finishes it feels clean and grippy and hassle free and also having a central symmetrical camera is nice and practical for using the thing on a flat surface however these cameras have become so big so deep and so weighty that it feels they ve started to invade the core phone experience to the point where most of the people i ve handed this to it s the first thing they notice and they are completely put off by it especially compared to the samsung which is so great for bright hued almost any position and still avoid the cameras to quote joseph in my team here it feels you re holding holding a piece of tech stuck onto a piece of tech and while i can tolerate it i feel i m having to exert extra grip strength just to keep it in my hands you feel the urge every single time you pick it up to hold it higher up than usual to balance out that weight distribution but back i hate it clearly this whole thin on the top thick on the sides thing is a purposeful decision that xia s made but i just don t understand why cuz to me it just looks they took this leather strip ve cleaned up the control center when you swipe down just ios they ve got rid of all the text that tells you what each icon is which means it takes a little bit of getting used to but once you re used to it it feels clean now you can fit more icons on screen and instead of swiping between pages you just scroll down now there s new animations and the whole thing is really fluid i only really clocked notifications in all cases with a little swipe down that feels so intuitive to me the customization is absolutely absurd thanks to samsung s goodlock suite of apps and you can do it all without any features.evidence: this phone is a flagship yet not the highest tier it s a camera phone yet it won t wow you with a set of zoom lenses and it chipset is super fast yet it s not qualcom i m will for gsm marina and this is the xiaomi 13t pro let s check it out the xiaomi 13t pro is a bit less expensive than xiaomi s top flagships but it still brings a full suite of premium features with both the xiaomi 13t and 13t pro a major focus is the cameras which have been developed in partnership with leica but compared to the other model the pro brings a top tier chipset 120 w charging 8k video capture and wi fi i7 connectivity when it comes to looks what grabs the eye is the huge square camera bump with leica branding you have the choice of either a glass or eco leather back and our pro model is in the blue leather the back curves slightly into a plastic frame which has a brushed finish that imitates metal the phone feels grippy and sturdy as well one nice inclusion is that the xiaomi 13t and 13t pro have ip68 rated dust and water resistance a first for the t series the display of the phone is quite premium it s a 6

67 in oled with a 1220p resolution gorilla glass 5 protection and a 144 hz refresh rate at 446 ppi this panel is quite sharp there are plenty of options when it comes to the colors and they can be very accurate and you also get goodies 12 bit color depth and support for hdr 10 plus video and dolby vision the brightness is also great the xiaomi 13t pro achieved a maximum of nearly 500 nits when controlled with a manual slider and this was able to boost up to 1100 nits in auto mode let s talk about the phone s high refresh rate 144 hzz is meant to provide even more smoothness on top of a 120 hz panel but we only saw this display going to 144 within certain apps or games on the home screen it would stick to 120 and when idling we saw it drop down to 60 hz to save energy the xiaomi 13t pro has a pair of stereo speakers with dolby atmos support they have good loudness and the sound quality is also good with nice vocals and some base you can hear for yourself by checking out the provided link you can wake up and unlock the xiaomi 13t pro using the under display fingerprint scanner it s quite fast and reliable and the phone can come with 256 or 512 gigs or a whole terabyte of storage that s not expandable through microsd the interface of the xiaomi 13t pro is the global version of mi14 on on top of android 13 in almost every way it s the same as what we ve seen on recent xiaomi phones but it s worth mentioning that xiaomi promises four years of major android updates and 5 years of security patches for the 13t series if you d a more in depth overview of mi14 you can check out our dedicated video the xiaomi 13t pro might bring a mediatech chipset but it s not a setup meant for a mid ranger this is the best mediatech chipset on the market the dimensity 9200 plus and it s fullon flagship silicon it supports advanced memory tech ufs 4

this phone is a flagship yet not the highest tier it s a camera phone yet it won t wow you with a set of zoom lenses and it chipset is super fast yet it s not qualcom i m will for gsm marina and this is the xiaomi 13t pro let s check it out the xiaomi 13t pro is a bit less expensive than xiaomi s top flagships but it still brings a full suite of premium features with both the xiaomi 13t and 13t pro a major focus is the cameras which have been developed in partnership with leica but compared to the other model the pro brings a top tier chipset 120 w charging 8k video capture and wi fi i7 connectivity when it comes to looks what grabs the eye is the huge square camera bump with leica branding you have the choice of either a glass or eco leather back and our pro model is in the blue leather the back curves slightly into a plastic frame which has a brushed finish that imitates metal the phone feels grippy and sturdy as well one nice inclusion is that the xiaomi 13t and 13t pro have ip68 rated dust and water resistance a first for the t series the display of the phone is quite premium it s a 6

features.evidence: foreign xiaomi s pocophone started off as flagship killers but with the poco f5 pro they ve moved into flagship territory at least price wise with a higher price comes higher expectations so is the poco f5 pro able to keep up with other high tier phones i m will for gsmarena and let s find out in our full review xiaomi is doing things a bit differently with this year s poco f5 pro it isn t aimed specifically at gamers and it comes at a price closer to a flagship than a mid ranger right off the bat though you d notice that the cameras are the same as those on the regular poco f5 despite the rearrangement of the camera bump on the back so how does the pro stand out from the mid range vanilla model there are indeed some upgrades here including a higher tier chipset a higher res screen a slightly larger battery and wireless charging also the f5 pro brings a glossy glass design the cameras are a neat accent and so are the stripes which run towards the plastic frame the phone feels nice in hand but a bit slippery there is ingress protection here rated at ip53 the same is on the regular f5 and other xiaomi mid rangers on the front the f5 pro s display has an extra sharp qhd resolution it s a 6

67 inch amoled with a 120hz refresh rate and gorilla glass fire protection besides the sharpness and the oled tech there are other goodies here as well support for 12 bit color dolby vision and hdr 10 plus video the colors can also be quite accurate based on the color settings you choose the max brightness here is also plenty for comfy use outdoors we measured a maximum of 520 nits with a manual brightness slider which can boost up to over a thousand nids in auto mode and the smooth 120hz refresh rate can dial down automatically to 60hz when idling or when playing certain content or apps you can wake up and unlock the poco f5 pro with an under display fingerprint reader a bit fancier than the side mounted one on the vanilla model but unlike the f5 you don t get a headphone jack here you d have to use an adapter for traditional headphones otherwise there s a pair of stereo speakers with dolby atmos support they have good loudness and the sound quality is decent with clean vocals and highs and a bit of bass foreign and the phone comes with 256 or 512 gigs of storage on board and that isn t expandable through microsd something which places the poco f5 pro into a higher tier than the regular f5 is this flagship grade snapper grenade plus gen 1 chipset but it s a modified version compared to the one you d find in last year s flagships this one has lower clock speeds sacrificing a bit of the peak performance for some theoretical energy savings there s no need to worry though in benchmarks the poco f5 pro still does a great job and keeps up with the phones running on the original version of the chipset the performance here is clearly flagship worthy however it s worth mentioning that the vanilla poco f5 with its new snapdroken 7 plus gen 2 nearly manages to keep pace with the pro the f5 pro features an advanced passive cooling system but in our prolonged stress tests as thermal management was just okay it maintains stable performance until a certain point when it started throttling heavily with repeated dips a behavior that could cause stutters while gaming the f5 pro s battery capacity is quite sizable at 5160 milliamp hours the battery life is decent but less than the f5s with an endurance rating of 99 hours and some subpar screen on time when web browsing the f5 pro supports 67 watt charging which is quite speedy able to charge the phone from 0 to 86 percent in half an hour their support for wireless charging too which you don t get on the vanilla model the interface of the new poco phones is xiaomi s latest miui 14 running on top of android 13
